post.title: China’s 2025 Harvest Just Redrew the Global Grain Map!!
post.excerpt: China’s latest agricultural milestone is not just a domestic victory; it is a seismic shift for the global grain trade. Official data from the National Bureau of Statistics (NBS) confirms that China’s 2025 grain output reached a record 714.88 million tonnes,...
post.body:
China’s latest agricultural milestone is not just a domestic victory; it is a seismic shift for the global grain trade. Official data from the National Bureau of Statistics (NBS) confirms that China’s 2025 grain output reached a record 714.88 million tonnes, marking a year-on-year increase of 8.4 million tonnes (1.2%). While the headline figure is impressive, the real story lies in the "how" and the "what"— specifically the unprecedented surge in corn productivity and the aggressive deployment of "AI + Agriculture." A Deep Dive into the Commodity Surge China has now maintained an output above 700 million tonnes for two consecutive years. The most explosive growth came from the corn sector, which reached a staggering 301.24 million tonnes. This 2.1% year-on-year increase was the primary engine of the harvest, accounting for a massive 75% of the total grain increment. In the staples category, rice production climbed to 209.04 million tonnes, a 0.7% rise driven by early rice varieties and high-yield hybrids. Wheat output remained remarkably resilient at 140.07 million tonnes, holding stable despite the summer weather volatility that threatened key growing regions. Meanwhile, soybean production reached 20.91 million tonnes, growing by 1.3% and successfully stabilizing above the critical 20-million-tonne threshold for the fourth consecutive year. The "Corn Engine" and Per-Unit Yields The most critical takeaway for market analysts is that 90% of this growth was driven by per-unit yield enhancements, rather than land expansion. This was achieved through the National Corn

Yield Improvement Project, which utilized the "dense planting precision regulated model". Furthermore, China’s Genetically Engineered (GE) crop acreage reached 2.7 million hectares in 2025, a massive jump from the previous year. The Technological Secret: AI & Smart Farming Beijing is no longer just "planting seeds"; it is "planting data." The "AI + Agriculture" program saw the iteration of large models for smart irrigation and precision nutrient management. According to the Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Affairs (MARA), the contribution rate of scientific and technological progress to agriculture now exceeds 64%. Global Trade Implications: The Import Slump For exporters in the US, Brazil, and Ukraine, these numbers are a warning. As domestic corn production hits the 300M tonne ceiling, China’s import dependency is plummeting. Corn Imports: Projected to fall to ~8 million tonnes, a dramatic retreat from the 30 million tonnes seen in 2023-24. Self-Sufficiency: The drive to reduce reliance on foreign "feed and oil" is working. While soybean imports remain high (~110M tonnes), the structural reliance on foreign corn and wheat is fading.
post.author: Know Your Commodity
